source_policy: Current official DHD model information and governed canonical profiles are the authority. Public family reflects intended design role. Construction variants retain the same fundamental family unless DHD describes a materially different design.

models.manufacturer_intent: DHD's true all-rounder with easy paddle and entry, broad condition range and enough performance for better waves.
models.editorial_reason: Everyday versatility and one-board-quiver intent are explicit.

models.manufacturer_intent: EPS construction of the Black Diamond all-rounder.
models.editorial_reason: Construction changes response, not the model's fundamental daily-driver identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Soft-top version of DHD's all-rounder with extra accessibility and flotation.
models.editorial_reason: It remains an all-round daily driver, with construction increasing forgiveness.

models.manufacturer_intent: Ethan Ewing competition design developed from the DNA family with more rocker for tighter turns and pocket surfing.
models.editorial_reason: World Tour competition intent and technical pocket performance make this a Performance Shortboard, not a Daily Driver.

models.manufacturer_intent: Junior-scaled version of Ethan Ewing's competition Juliette design.
models.editorial_reason: Scaling and EPS construction do not change the performance-shortboard identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Easy-paddling mid-length for smooth lines, tighter turns and broad one-to-six-foot use.
models.editorial_reason: DHD explicitly defines it as its version of a mid length.

models.manufacturer_intent: Lively, easy-to-surf all-rounder for younger surfers, derived from the MF Lightning.
models.editorial_reason: Versatile everyday performance is the core role.

models.manufacturer_intent: Mick Fanning's competition board with performance rocker, drive and precision for rippable quality waves.
models.editorial_reason: It is explicitly DHD's competition performance reference.

models.manufacturer_intent: Junior-scaled MF DNA high-performance shortboard.
models.editorial_reason: The junior format retains the DNA competition role.

models.manufacturer_intent: Faster, more everyday evolution of the MF DNA for smaller and weaker surf while retaining performance.
models.editorial_reason: DHD positions it as the broader everyday companion to the DNA.

models.manufacturer_intent: Modern high-performance twin designed to feel familiar to thruster surfers, with greater control and range than a traditional twin.
models.editorial_reason: DHD explicitly states it is not a retro fish and is built to be pushed like a performance shortboard.

models.manufacturer_intent: Short, rounded retro-inspired twin for one-to-four-foot surf with modern control.
models.editorial_reason: Small-wave twin-fin nostalgia and compact outline place it in Fish.

models.manufacturer_intent: Shorter, wider swallow-tail twin with channels, designed for lively summer surfing in one-to-three-foot waves.
models.editorial_reason: Twin-fin fish outline and summer-wave role are primary, rather than generic daily-driver use.

models.manufacturer_intent: Explosive small-wave design for beach breaks and wave pools, combining easy acceleration with tight pocket response in one-to-four-foot surf.
models.editorial_reason: Weak-wave and wave-pool performance are the explicit primary role.

models.manufacturer_intent: EPS construction of the Nexus small-wave and wave-pool design.
models.editorial_reason: Construction does not change the Nexus's groveller identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Shorter, higher-volume board for one-to-four-foot surf, designed to create speed and performance below normal shortboard conditions.
models.editorial_reason: The model is explicitly sized and intended as a small-wave performance board.

models.manufacturer_intent: Swallow-tail EPS Phoenix for one-to-four-foot performance surfing.
models.editorial_reason: The Phoenix small-wave role remains primary.

models.manufacturer_intent: Refined Phoenix evolution retaining weak-wave speed while adding a sleeker outline, tighter turns and more control as surf gains push.
models.editorial_reason: It bridges small-wave performance and broader everyday range; it is not a Step Up despite the previous inferred label.

models.manufacturer_intent: Easy-surfing board for normal surfers with enough range to serve as a semi step-up for more regular surfers.
models.editorial_reason: Ease and all-round use are primary; semi-step-up capability is secondary.

models.manufacturer_intent: Stephanie Gilmore high-performance shortboard for speed, flow, precision and two-to-six-foot surf.
models.editorial_reason: DHD calls it an all-round high-performance shortboard; it is not a Step Up.

models.manufacturer_intent: Travel step-up with refined rails, narrower nose and tail, stronger paddling and control for heavier conditions.
models.editorial_reason: DHD explicitly calls it the ultimate step up and travel board.

models.manufacturer_intent: Modern twin-fin design for summer fun across one-to-five-foot beach, point and reef waves.
models.editorial_reason: Twin-fin summer design and small-wave speed place it in Fish rather than generic Daily Driver.
